zh.lifeSummary: 當George Wallis於1619年出生在英國倫敦時，他的父親Ralph Wallen29歲，母親Mrs. Ralph Wallis20歲。他於1645年與Eleanor Ann Wallis在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1685年12月14日在美國新罕布夏州洛金哈姆波斯姆斯享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当George Wallis于1619年出生在英国倫敦时，他的父亲Ralph Wallen29岁，母亲Mrs. Ralph Wallis20岁。他于1645年与Eleanor Ann Wallis在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加劍橋大學默里 哈普爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1685年12月14日在美国新罕布什尔州洛金哈姆波斯姆斯享年66岁。

nameMeanings.name: Wallis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: from Anglo-Norman French Waleis, Walais ‘Welshman’, also sometimes ‘Breton’ (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning a ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). In western and central England the medieval reference is clearly to Welshmen. In Norfolk and Lincolnshire, the reference was probably to Bretons, many of whom settled in the eastern counties after the Conquest. Compare Welsh , Walsh . The idea that the Scottish surname refers to the Welsh-speaking Britons of Strathclyde is erroneous.

variants.name: Wallace
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Wallis  , especially in Scotland, where the name was introduced from the Welsh Marches by a family of tenants of the Stewarts in the 12th century.
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, e.g.   Wallach  .

variants.name: Wales
meanings: habitational name from Wales near Sheffield (South Yorkshire). The placename means ‘The Welshmen’ and denoted a settlement of Brittonic speakers living on the boundary between the Anglo-Saxon kingdoms of Northumbria and Mercia (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). See also Wallis .
habitational name from Wales, the country named from Old English wēalas ‘foreigners’ (especially those speaking Celtic or Romance languages). This meaning of the surname is quite rare.

variants.name: Welsh
meanings: Scottish, English, and Irish: ethnic name from Middle English welshe (Old English wēalisc, wēlisc) ‘foreign, Welsh’, for someone from Wales or a speaker of the Welsh language. Compare Walsh and Wallace .
Americanized form of Ukrainian and Jewish (from Ukraine) Voloshin or its Polish variant Wołoszyn (see Woloszyn ).

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When George Wallis was born in 1619, in London, England, his father, Ralph Wallen, was 29 and his mother, Mrs. Ralph Wallis, was 20. He married Eleanor Ann Wallis in 1645, in New Hampshire, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 14 December 1685, in Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, United States, at the age of 66.

Il a épousé Eleanor Ann Wallis en 1645, à New Hampshire, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 14 décembre 1685, à Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, États-Unis, à l’âge de 66 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando George Wallis è nato il nel 1619 nel London, England, il suo padre, Ralph Wallen, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Ralph Wallis, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor Ann Wallis nel 1645, nel New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 14 dicembre 1685, nel Portsmouth, Rockingham, New Hampshire, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 66 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: George Wallisが1619年にイギリスロンドンで生まれた時，父Ralph Wallenは29歳，母Mrs. Ralph Wallisは20歳であった。1645年にイギリス植民地アメリカニュー ハンプシュアイアでEleanor Ann Wallisと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1685年12月14日にアメリカ合衆国ニューハンプシャー州ロクインガムポルトスモウスで66歳で死去した。

ko.Casco, Cumberland, Massachusetts Bay Col: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 c벌랜드 카스코

ko.Plymouth, Plymouth, Massachusetts Bay Co: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 플림우스 플림우스
ko.lifeSummary: George Wallis(이)가 1619년 영국 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph Wallen(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Ralph Wallis(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1645년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 함프실르에서 Eleanor Ann Wallis(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1685년 12월 14일 미국 뉴햄프셔 주 록잉함 포츠모우스에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando George Wallis nasceu em 1619, em London, Inglaterra, seu pai, Ralph Wallen, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Ralph Wallis, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor Ann Wallis em 1645, em New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 14 de dezembro de 1685, em Portsmouth, Rockingham, Nova Hampshire, Estados Unidos da América, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Wallis родился в 1619 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, его отцу по имени Ralph Wallen было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Ralph Wallis, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor Ann Wallis в 1645 г. в местности ню хампшир, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 14 декабря 1685 г. в местности порцмоут, роккингхам, Нью-Гэмпшир, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 66 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando George Wallis nació en 1619, en London, Inglaterra, su papá, Ralph Wallen, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Mrs. Ralph Wallis, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Eleanor Ann Wallis en 1645, en New Hampshire, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 14 de diciembre de 1685, en Portsmouth, Rockingham, Nuevo Hampshire, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 66 años.
